The function `longest_common_subsequence` finds the longest common subsequence of two strings using dynamic programming. It first initializes a 2D array `dp` to store the lengths of common subsequences. Then it fills up the `dp` array by comparing characters from the two strings. Finally, it constructs the longest common subsequence by backtracking from the bottom-right corner of the `dp` array.

The function longest_common_subsequence(s1, s2) finds the longest common subsequence of two strings s1 and s2. It uses dynamic programming to build a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] is the length of the longest common subsequence of the first i characters of s1 and the first j characters of s2. It then backtracks through the dp array to construct the longest common subsequence.
